The rich prophetic legacy of my dear brother Rev. Jesse Jackson Sr. must not be confined to the mediocre Democratic Party -- social movements of liberation go beyond the corrupt two-party system! #TruthJusticeLove @tavissmiley @MichaelPfleger @RStevenBJacobs @kbla1580

posts.text: On Friday we honored the life and witness of Jesse Jackson Sr. a freedom fighter who reminded America that justice is what love looks like in public! He stood in the great prophetic tradition—telling the truth, lifting up the poor, and insisting that every child of God has dignity! His voice carried the cry of the marginalized and the hope of a more compassionate democracy! As we reflect today, may we remember that his legacy is not just in history but in the ongoing struggle for spiritual integ

Martin Luther King Jr. said, “Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere,” reminding us that as we consistently condemn war, we must also affirm that people have the right to resist oppression and stand against unjust regimes! #TruthJusticeLove @tavissmiley @kbla1580

Video

We’re not just facing a political crisis — we’re facing a spiritual one! When greed is normalized, truth is disposable, and cruelty is marketed as strength, the soul of the nation is at stake! This isn’t about one man. It’s about a moral decay that rewards corruption and punishes integrity. We must

posts.text:
Brother Newsom could’ve taken a humanistic approach! He could’ve said, “I’m just like you. I was born of a woman. I’ll face loss, pain, and death just like anyone else.” But instead, when he thinks of Black people, he brings up low SAT scores. That mindset sits at the core of white supremacy—the belief that Black people are less beautiful, less moral, less intelligent! Don’t play with us like that @CAgovernor !! 
#TruthJusticeLove
@tavissmiley @kbla1580

Video

Painful moral truths in a narrow ideological-driven world must be heard and heeded! #TruthJusticeLove


Annahita Mahdavi West آناهيتا (@AnnahitaMahdavi)

I received these messages from Iran as some internet connections have come back online.

In the past few weeks, while tens of thousands of Iranians have been slaughtered, countless more have been arrested, disappeared, and erased. Families are screaming into the void while the world looks away.
